title: Complete Guide to EV Maintenance in India
excerpt: Discover everything you need to know about maintaining your electric vehicle in Indian conditions. Learn about battery care, service schedules, and cost savings.
content:
Electric vehicles are transforming the automotive landscape in India. With the government's push towards electric mobility and increasing fuel prices, more Indians are making the switch to EVs. However, maintaining an electric vehicle differs significantly from traditional ICE vehicles.

Battery Care Tips

The battery is the heart of your electric vehicle. To maximize its lifespan, avoid charging to 100% regularly. Keep it between 20-80% for daily use. In India's hot climate, park in shaded areas when possible.

Service Schedule

Unlike petrol or diesel vehicles, EVs require minimal maintenance. No oil changes, fewer brake replacements due to regenerative braking, and simpler drivetrains mean lower maintenance costs.

Cost Savings

EV owners in India save significantly on fuel costs. With electricity rates much lower than petrol prices, you can save up to 70% on running costs compared to traditional vehicles.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: How Long Do EV Batteries Last?
excerpt: Learn everything about EV battery lifespan, factors affecting battery life, and tips to maximize your electric vehicle battery performance.
content:
One of the most common questions potential EV buyers ask is about battery longevity. Modern EV batteries are designed to last the lifetime of the vehicle, typically 8-10 years or more.

Battery Degradation

EV batteries gradually lose capacity over time, but the rate is much slower than many people fear. Most manufacturers guarantee at least 70% capacity after 8 years or 160,000 km.

Factors Affecting Battery Life

Extreme temperatures, frequent fast charging, and keeping the battery at 100% charge for extended periods can accelerate degradation. In India's climate, proper thermal management is crucial.

Replacement Costs

While battery replacement costs have been falling rapidly, they still represent a significant expense. However, with proper care, most owners won't need a replacement during their ownership period.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: EV Charging Infrastructure in India: 2024 Guide
excerpt: Explore the current state of EV charging infrastructure across India. Find charging stations, understand charging speeds, and plan your EV journeys.
content:
India's EV charging infrastructure is expanding rapidly. With government initiatives and private investments, finding a charging station is becoming easier than ever in major cities and highways.

Types of Charging

Level 1 (slow AC charging) is suitable for home use. Level 2 (fast AC) is common at public stations. DC fast charging can replenish 80% battery in 30-60 minutes, perfect for long trips.

Charging Networks

Major networks include Tata Power EZ Charge, Ather Grid, and BPCL's charging stations. Many malls, hotels, and offices now offer charging facilities.

Home Charging

Most EV owners charge at home overnight. A standard 16A socket can fully charge most electric two-wheelers in 4-5 hours. Installing a wallbox charger can speed this up significantly.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: Top 5 Electric Scooters in India 2024
excerpt: Compare the best electric scooters available in India. Features, pricing, range, and performance analysis to help you choose the right EV for your needs.
content:
The electric scooter market in India is booming with excellent options from both established manufacturers and new entrants. Here's our analysis of the top models.

Ola S1 Pro

With a range of 181 km and top speed of 116 km/h, the Ola S1 Pro offers impressive performance. The 7-inch touchscreen and multiple riding modes add to its appeal.

Ather 450X

Known for its build quality and performance, the Ather 450X offers a true range of 105 km. The Ather Grid charging network is a significant advantage.

TVS iQube

TVS's electric offering provides a reliable 100 km range with the backing of a trusted service network, making it a practical choice for many buyers.

Bajaj Chetak

The iconic Chetak name returns as an electric scooter with premium build quality and a range of 95 km. Its retro styling appeals to many buyers.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: EV Service Franchise Opportunity in India
excerpt: Learn about the growing EV service market and franchise opportunities. Start your own EV service business with Aerth Mobility's complete support.
content:
The electric vehicle revolution in India is creating a massive opportunity in the service and maintenance sector. As EV adoption grows, so does the need for skilled service providers.

Market Opportunity

With millions of EVs expected on Indian roads by 2030, the service market will be worth billions. Early entrants can establish strong market positions.

Franchise Benefits

Aerth Mobility provides comprehensive training, equipment support, technical assistance, and marketing help. Our franchise partners benefit from our established brand and expertise.

Investment Requirements

Starting an EV service franchise requires less investment than traditional automotive service centers. No need for expensive exhaust systems or oil handling equipment.

Training Program

Our intensive training covers EV diagnostics, battery systems, motor controllers, and safety protocols. Continuous support ensures franchisees stay updated with latest technologies.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: Common EV Problems and Solutions
excerpt: Troubleshoot common electric vehicle issues. From charging problems to software glitches, learn how to identify and resolve typical EV concerns.
content:
While electric vehicles are generally more reliable than ICE vehicles, they can encounter specific issues. Understanding these problems helps in quick resolution.

Charging Issues

If your EV won't charge, check the power source, charging cable connections, and ensure the charging port is clean. Software updates sometimes resolve charging protocol issues.

Reduced Range

Sudden range reduction often indicates battery calibration issues or tire pressure problems. Extreme temperatures also temporarily affect range.

Software Glitches

Modern EVs rely heavily on software. Display freezes, app connectivity issues, and feature malfunctions usually resolve with a system restart or update.

Brake System

While EVs use regenerative braking, traditional brakes are still present. Unusual noises might indicate brake pad wear or regenerative braking system calibration needs.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: Understanding EV Battery Health Metrics
excerpt: Learn to interpret battery health indicators, state of health (SOH), and capacity metrics. Monitor your EV battery's condition like a pro.
content:
Battery health is crucial for EV performance and resale value. Understanding key metrics helps you maintain your battery optimally and identify issues early.

State of Health (SOH)

SOH represents your battery's current capacity compared to when it was new. A SOH of 90% means the battery retains 90% of its original capacity. Most EVs show this in their apps.

State of Charge (SOC)

SOC indicates current battery charge level. Unlike SOH which degrades slowly, SOC changes constantly during use and charging.

Cell Balance

EV batteries consist of thousands of cells. Good battery management systems maintain cell balance for optimal performance and longevity.

Temperature Management

Battery temperature significantly affects performance and health. Modern EVs have thermal management systems to maintain optimal temperatures.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: EV Tax Benefits and Subsidies in India 2024
excerpt: Maximize your savings with government incentives for electric vehicles. Complete guide to FAME II subsidies, state benefits, and tax advantages.
content:
The Indian government offers substantial incentives to promote electric vehicle adoption. Understanding these benefits can significantly reduce your EV purchase and running costs.

FAME II Subsidy

The Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Electric Vehicles scheme offers direct subsidies on EV purchases. Two-wheelers can get up to ₹15,000 subsidy depending on battery capacity.

State Incentives

States like Delhi, Maharashtra, Gujarat, and Tamil Nadu offer additional benefits including registration fee waivers, road tax exemptions, and purchase subsidies.

Income Tax Benefits

Section 80EEB allows deduction of up to ₹1.5 lakh on interest paid for EV loans. This benefit is available for both personal and commercial vehicle purchases.

Reduced GST

Electric vehicles attract only 5% GST compared to 28% for petrol/diesel vehicles and 12% for hybrid vehicles, making them significantly cheaper.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: Fleet Electrification Guide for Businesses
excerpt: Transition your business fleet to electric vehicles. Cost analysis, charging infrastructure planning, and implementation strategies for successful electrification.
content:
Businesses across India are increasingly electrifying their fleets to reduce operating costs and meet sustainability goals. Here's a comprehensive guide to successful fleet electrification.

Total Cost of Ownership

While EVs have higher upfront costs, they offer significant savings in fuel and maintenance. Most businesses see ROI within 2-3 years of fleet electrification.

Charging Infrastructure

Installing workplace charging is crucial. Plan for overnight charging for vehicles that return to base, and public charging network access for vehicles on the move.

Vehicle Selection

Match vehicles to use cases. Last-mile delivery might need different EVs than inter-city transport. Range requirements and payload capacity are key considerations.

Driver Training

EVs require different driving techniques for optimal efficiency. Regenerative braking, eco-mode usage, and charging habits need to be part of driver training programs.
author: Aerth Mobility

title: The Future of Electric Mobility in India
excerpt: Explore upcoming EV technologies, market trends, and the roadmap for electric mobility in India. What's next for the EV revolution?
content:
India's electric mobility sector is poised for explosive growth. With government targets of 30% EV penetration by 2030, the next few years will see massive transformation.

Battery Technology Advances

Solid-state batteries promise higher energy density, faster charging, and improved safety. Several manufacturers plan to introduce these in the Indian market by 2025-2026.

Charging Infrastructure Expansion

The government plans to install charging stations every 25 km on highways. Urban areas will see charging points at petrol pumps, malls, and residential complexes.

Local Manufacturing

PLI schemes are attracting global battery and EV manufacturers to set up in India. This will reduce costs and create a robust local supply chain.

Two and Three-Wheeler Revolution

India's unique position as a major two-wheeler market is driving innovation in affordable electric scooters and motorcycles. Price parity with petrol vehicles is expected by 2025.
author: Aerth Mobility
